title: Coordinates, Graphs, Functions, and Rates of Change · Assumption Clinic
summary: Model water use, travel, cost, and capacity from tables, graphs, formulas, and descriptions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a multi-representation resource dashboard and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plot and interpret ordered pairs with axis quantities, units, origin, and scale.
Determine whether a relationship is a function and define practical input and output domains.
Translate a relationship among words, tables, graphs, and formulas while preserving meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Intercept Domain for a multi-representation resource dashboard.
steps.why: The Intercept Domain result for a multi-representation resource dashboard is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret intercepts and restrict conclusions to the meaningful domain of a model.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Intercept Domain, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a multi-representation resource dashboard. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret intercepts and restrict conclusions to the meaningful domain of a model.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
